ddls:
CREATE TABLE "patients" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient */,
  "isolate_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the isolate collected from the patient */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the patient */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information of the patient */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Exact date of birth for more precise age calculations */,
  "address" TEXT /* Patient's address for geographic analysis */,
  "medical_history" TEXT /* Summary of the patient's medical history */,
  "treatment_history" TEXT /* Summary of the patient's treatment history */,
  "allergies" TEXT /* List of known allergies */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Patient's occupation for demographic analysis */,
  "travel_history" TEXT /* Recent travel history to identify potential exposure to different resistance patterns */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "antibiotics" (
  "antibiotic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each antibiotic */,
  "drug_name" TEXT /* Name of the antibiotic */,
  "class" TEXT /* Class of the antibiotic */,
  "breakpoint_values" TEXT /* Breakpoint values for determining susceptibility */,
  "mechanism_of_action" TEXT /* Description of how the antibiotic works */,
  "side_effects" TEXT /* Common side effects of the antibiotic */,
  "dosage_guidelines" TEXT /* Recommended dosage guidelines */,
  "approval_date" TEXT /* Date when the antibiotic was approved for use */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the antibiotic */,
  "availability" TEXT /* Availability status (e.g., over-the-counter, prescription-only) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("antibiotic_id")
)
column_names: Reference to the isolate collected from the patient
Exact date of birth for more precise age calculations
Summary of the patient's medical history
Summary of the patient's treatment history
Recent travel history to identify potential exposure to different resistance patterns
Description of how the antibiotic works
Common side effects of the antibiotic
Date when the antibiotic was approved for use
